The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ambrosia scariosa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ambrosia scariosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
